title: microseris laciniata
intro: Microseris laciniata, also known as the laciniated false dandelion or prairie dandelion, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the northwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve constipation, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Microseris laciniata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and act as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of Microseris laciniata can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for use in treating various health conditions.
